mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicate robust consumer demand outpacing supply constraints, which forces equity markets to reprice earnings upward and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yields negative or flat SPX returns in >60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals resilient manufacturing output that overrides rate hike concerns, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles fail to generate positive 5-day SPX returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which mechanically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions correlate with negative SPX 20d returns in >55% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a collapse in construction demand and broader credit tightening, which triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as financials and industrials underperform.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of months where HOUST falls below 1200.0.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative and erode purchasing power, prompting equity market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS growth consistently yield positive SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms economic stabilization, which reduces tail risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 values above 2.5% annualized growth fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in >50% of post-recession quarters.
